title: Curator of Entropy
description: The final Masterite of the abstract world. The Curator of Entropy has been watching all six worlds unravel toward their inevitable conclusion.

lines: The Curator of Entropy was not a dramatic presence. It was a quiet one.
It sat where a throne might have been if the abstract world had needed thrones. It sat where the center of a space would be if the abstract world had geometry.
It looked at the party the way something looks at things it has been watching for a very long time.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Everything ends. I merely accelerate the inevitable.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: That's the most honest thing any of you have said.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I have had a long time to practice honesty. The Curator of the Flame had fire. The Curator of the Budget had ledgers. The Curator of the Pipeline had flow. I have time, and time is honest.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You have fought very hard to persist. I respect this in a purely observational sense. All things fight to persist. It is the nature of organized systems to resist their own dissolution.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I am the dissolution. I do not choose it. I am what happens when the choosing is done.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You have passed the Warden, the Arbiter, the Tempo. You have reached the end of the system. I am what the system deposits here when it has nothing else left to throw.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Then something persists that I could not dissolve. That would be the most interesting thing that has happened in a very long time.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I have not decided whether I want you to pass. I find I am curious, which is not a sensation I expected to have at this stage.
